summary: Iteration 5: comprehensive test of new PlacementPrep upgrades — Aptitude Category→Topic→Subtopic→Difficulty→Mode flow (52 topics), smart question pipeline (seed+cache+Gemini), AI Chatbot (Gemini 2.5 Pro), and Developer profile page. Backend 9/9 pytest tests passed. Frontend full E2E flow worked: login → /aptitude (3 categories with correct counts 27/15/10) → Quantitative → 27 topics → search filter 'time' → click Percentage → subtopic view → Skip → Easy difficulty (emerald badge) → Practice mode → questions render with 'AI' badge for AI-generated questions. Chatbot floating button opens panel with personalised greeting ('Hi Test...'); Developer page renders Ayush Jain profile with AJ monogram, social buttons, tech stack, vision cards. Zero console / page errors during the flow.

issues: Welcome back! toast at bottom-right can briefly overlap the floating chatbot button immediately after login. Consider offsetting the chatbot button up by ~80px while a toast is visible, or auto-dismissing toasts faster.

action_items: (Low priority) Adjust z-index/position so 'Welcome back' toast does not visually overlap chatbot floating button on dashboard.
(Nit) Backend test_chatbot_message_fresh_session has a stray 'return data["session_id"]' that triggers PytestReturnNotNoneWarning — non-blocking.
critical_code_review_comments: ai_service.py: chatbot_reply inlines the last 12 history turns into the system prompt instead of maintaining LlmChat state — pragmatic and fast, but for very long sessions consider summarising older turns to stay within the model context window.
question_service._store_cache uses per-document upsert in a loop — fine for current count buffer (~5-7 docs) but consider bulk_write with UpdateOne for larger batches.

server.py is now ~1000 lines with mixed concerns (auth, catalog, questions, chatbot, attempts, interviews, uploads, admin). Recommend splitting into routers/*.py modules — pure refactor, not blocking.
topic_catalog.get_topic / get_category return None implicitly via .get(); type hints declare Dict[str, Any] (not Optional). Update annotations to Optional[Dict[...]].

Chatbot.js: localStorage session_id is shared across users on the same browser. If user A logs out and user B logs in, B will load A's session_id and call /chatbot/history (returns empty for B but the stored id leaks). Suggest namespacing the key with user.id or clearing on logout.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing test user. test_iter5.py auto-signs up if login fails.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All new iter5 features (catalog, /questions/topic with cache+Gemini, /chatbot/*, attempt-by-hash, Developer page, Chatbot widget) are working. test_iter5.py is the regression suite for these. The Gemini key is real (EMERGENT_LLM_KEY) so chatbot replies and boats-streams generation are actual LLM calls (~10-15s on cold first call; cache hit <2s). Older flows (auth, ReasoningModule, basic Aptitude) were validated in iter4 and untouched.
